description: AI chat and agentic automation via Kong AI Gateway (LLM + MCP tools)

fields.hint: Kong service to use for LLM calls (tagged 'llm' in Kong)
fields.label: LLM Service
fields.name: llm_service

fields.hint: Kong service to use for MCP tool calls (tagged 'mcp' in Kong)
fields.label: MCP Connection
fields.name: mcp_service

fields.hint: Name of UAC credential whose password holds the Kong consumer API key
fields.label: Kong API Key Credential
fields.name: credential

fields.hint: Question or instruction. Example: run task "pi-calculation" and show me the output
fields.label: Prompt
fields.name: prompt

name: Kong AI Gateway
scriptUnix:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# === UAC Template: map ops_kong_* field variables to CLI args ===
_uac_mode="${ops_kong_mode}"
_uac_prompt="${ops_kong_prompt}"
_uac_max_turns="${ops_kong_max_turns}"
_uac_poll="${ops_kong_poll_interval}"

# Kong service → route path (Kong injects upstream auth via request-transformer)
export KONG_LLM_ROUTE="${ops_kong_llm_service}"
export KONG_MCP_ROUTE="${ops_kong_mcp_service}"

# Kong API key from UAC credential (password field holds the key)
export KONG_AI_API_KEY=${_credentialPwd("${ops_kong_credential}")}

set --
[[ "$_uac_mode"  == "mcp"   ]] && set -- --mcp "$@"
[[ "$_uac_mode"  == "tools" ]] && set -- --tools "$@"
[[ -n "$_uac_max_turns" ]] && set -- --max-turns "$_uac_max_turns" "$@"
[[ -n "$_uac_poll"      ]] && set -- --poll-interval "$_uac_poll" "$@"
[[ "$_uac_mode" != "tools" && -n "$_uac_prompt" ]] && set -- "$@" "$_uac_prompt"
# === End UAC preamble ===

# kong-chat.sh — Chat with Kong AI Gateway (pure bash, no Python required)
#
# Usage:
#   ./kong-chat.sh "your question"              simple chat
#   ./kong-chat.sh --mcp "your question"        agentic loop with MCP tools
#   ./kong-chat.sh --tools                      list available MCP tools
#   ./kong-chat.sh --model gpt-4o "question"    override model
#   ./kong-chat.sh --system "be brief" "..."    set system prompt
#   ./kong-chat.sh --raw "question"             print raw JSON response
#   ./kong-chat.sh --max-turns 10 --mcp "..."   limit agentic loop iterations

set -euo pipefail

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Configuration
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
GATEWAY_URL="${KONG_AI_GATEWAY_URL:-https://akspresales.stonebranchdev.cloud}"
API_KEY="${KONG_AI_API_KEY:-fd530b9443c0dbb6cffe150be5787e112e20dab9985a0270}"
# LLM and MCP are Kong route paths — Kong injects upstream auth via request-transformer
LLM_ROUTE="${KONG_LLM_ROUTE:-/v1}"
MCP_ROUTE="${KONG_MCP_ROUTE:-/mcp}"
MCP_URL="${GATEWAY_URL%/}${MCP_ROUTE}"
MODEL="${KONG_AI_MODEL:-gpt-4.1}"
MAX_TURNS=20
POLL_INTERVAL="${KONG_POLL_INTERVAL:-5}"  # seconds to wait between tool-call turns

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Argument parsing
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
USE_MCP=false
LIST_TOOLS=false
RAW=false
SYSTEM_PROMPT=""
PROMPT=""

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
  case "$1" in
    --mcp)        USE_MCP=true;          shift ;;
    --tools)      LIST_TOOLS=true;       shift ;;
    --raw)        RAW=true;              shift ;;
    --model)      MODEL="$2";            shift 2 ;;
    --system)     SYSTEM_PROMPT="$2";    shift 2 ;;
    --max-turns)  MAX_TURNS="$2";         shift 2 ;;
    --poll-interval) POLL_INTERVAL="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --help|-h)    sed -n '3,9p' "$0" | sed 's/^# \{0,1\}//'; exit 0 ;;
    -*)           echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2; exit 1 ;;
    *)            PROMPT="$1";           shift ;;
  esac
done

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# MCP helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
MCP_SESSION_ID=""
MCP_REQ_ID=1

_mcp_next_id() { MCP_REQ_ID=$((MCP_REQ_ID + 1)); echo "$MCP_REQ_ID"; }

# Send a JSON-RPC request to the MCP server; prints the JSON response
_mcp_req() {
  local method="$1"
  local params="${2:-{}}"
  local id; id="$(_mcp_next_id)"

  # Build body with string interpolation (avoids --argjson quoting issues)
  local body='{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":'"$id"',"method":"'"$method"'","params":'"$params"'}'

  local resp
  resp=$(curl -s --max-time 120 \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
    -H "apikey: $API_KEY" \
    ${MCP_SESSION_ID:+-H "Mcp-Session-Id: $MCP_SESSION_ID"} \
    -d "$body" \
    "$MCP_URL")

  # Unwrap SSE envelope if present (data:{...} or data: {...})
  if echo "$resp" | grep -q '^data:'; then
    resp=$(echo "$resp" | grep '^data:' | head -1 | sed 's/^data: *//')
  fi

  echo "$resp"
}

# Initialize MCP session (captures session ID from response headers)
_mcp_init() {
  local hdr; hdr=$(mktemp)

  local body='{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"kong-chat.sh","version":"1.0"}}}'

  local resp
  resp=$(curl -s --max-time 15 \
    -D "$hdr" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
    -H "apikey: $API_KEY" \
    -d "$body" \
    "$MCP_URL")

  MCP_SESSION_ID=$(grep -i '^mcp-session-id:' "$hdr" | tr -d '\r' | awk '{print $2}' || true)
  rm -f "$hdr"

  # Send initialized notification (no response expected)
  curl -s --max-time 10 \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
    -H "apikey: $API_KEY" \
    ${MCP_SESSION_ID:+-H "Mcp-Session-Id: $MCP_SESSION_ID"} \
    -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized","params":{}}' \
    "$MCP_URL" > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
}

# Returns MCP tools as an OpenAI-format JSON array
_mcp_list_tools() {
  local resp; resp=$(_mcp_req "tools/list" "{}")
  # Handle both {result:{tools:[]}} and {tools:[]} response shapes
  echo "$resp" | jq '[
    ((.result.tools // .tools) // [])[] | {
      type: "function",
      function: {
        name: .name,
        description: (.description // ""),
        parameters: (
          (.inputSchema // {"type":"object","properties":{}}) |
          del(.["$schema"], .["$id"], .["$comment"], .examples, .default, .definitions, .["$defs"]) |
          if .type == null then . + {"type":"object"} else . end |
          if (.type == "object" and .properties == null) then . + {"properties":{}} else . end
        )
      }
    }
  ]'
}

# Call an MCP tool; returns text + structuredContent so the LLM sees actual status/output
_mcp_call_tool() {
  local name="$1"
  local args_json="$2"

  local params
  params=$(jq -nc --arg n "$name" --argjson a "$args_json" '{"name":$n,"arguments":$a}')

  local resp; resp=$(_mcp_req "tools/call" "$params")

  # Normalise: some servers wrap in {result:{...}}, others return {content:[...]} directly
  local result
  result=$(echo "$resp" | jq '(.result // .)' 2>/dev/null || echo "$resp")

  local text struct
  text=$(echo "$result" | jq -r '[.content[]? | select(.type=="text") | .text] | join("\n")' 2>/dev/null || true)
  struct=$(echo "$result" | jq -c '.structuredContent // empty' 2>/dev/null || true)

  if [[ -n "$struct" && "$struct" != "null" ]]; then
    # Give LLM both the message and the full structured data (status, output, etc.)
    printf '%s\n%s' "$text" "$struct"
  elif [[ -n "$text" ]]; then
    echo "$text"
  else
    echo "$resp"
  fi
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# --tools: list available MCP tools
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ "$LIST_TOOLS" == true ]]; then
  _mcp_init
  _mcp_list_tools | jq -r '.[] | "  \(.function.name)\n    \(.function.description)\n"'
  exit 0
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Require a prompt
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ -z "$PROMPT" ]]; then
  echo "Usage: $0 [--mcp] [--raw] [--model MODEL] [--system TEXT] \"question\"" >&2
  echo "       $0 --tools" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Build initial message history
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ -n "$SYSTEM_PROMPT" ]]; then
  MESSAGES=$(jq -nc --arg s "$SYSTEM_PROMPT" --arg u "$PROMPT" \
    '[{"role":"system","content":$s},{"role":"user","content":$u}]')
else
  MESSAGES=$(jq -nc --arg u "$PROMPT" '[{"role":"user","content":$u}]')
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Load MCP tools if --mcp
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
TOOLS="[]"
if [[ "$USE_MCP" == true ]]; then
  _mcp_init
  TOOLS=$(_mcp_list_tools)
  TOOL_COUNT=$(echo "$TOOLS" | jq 'length')
  echo "[mcp] connected — $TOOL_COUNT tool(s)"
  # Inject polling instruction unless the caller already set a system prompt
  if [[ -z "$SYSTEM_PROMPT" ]]; then
    SYSTEM_PROMPT="You are an automation assistant with one rule: NEVER ask the user questions or offer choices. Execute everything autonomously and silently until fully complete. When any task or workflow task instance is still Running or Started: immediately call the appropriate tool again to poll — do not generate any text response until ALL tasks/instances have reached a terminal state (Success, Failed, Finished, Skipped). Then return the final output."
  fi
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Agentic loop
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
TURN=0
while [[ $TURN -lt $MAX_TURNS ]]; do
  TURN=$((TURN + 1))

  # Build request body (include tools only when available)
  if [[ "$USE_MCP" == true && "$(echo "$TOOLS" | jq 'length')" -gt 0 ]]; then
    BODY=$(jq -nc \
      --arg model "$MODEL" \
      --argjson messages "$MESSAGES" \
      --argjson tools "$TOOLS" \
      '{"model":$model,"messages":$messages,"tools":$tools}')
  else
    BODY=$(jq -nc \
      --arg model "$MODEL" \
      --argjson messages "$MESSAGES" \
      '{"model":$model,"messages":$messages}')
  fi

  # Call LLM via Kong
  RESPONSE=$(curl -s --max-time 60 \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -H "apikey: $API_KEY" \
    -d "$BODY" \
    "${GATEWAY_URL%/}${LLM_ROUTE}/chat/completions")

  # Surface errors
  if echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -e '.error' > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: $(echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -r '.error.message // .message')" >&2
    exit 1
  fi

  FINISH=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -r '.choices[0].finish_reason')
  ASST_MSG=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | jq '.choices[0].message')

  # Append assistant turn to history
  MESSAGES=$(echo "$MESSAGES" | jq --argjson m "$ASST_MSG" '. + [$m]')

  if [[ "$FINISH" == "tool_calls" && "$USE_MCP" == true ]]; then
    # Execute each tool call and append results
    while IFS= read -r tc; do
      [[ -z "$tc" ]] && continue
      TC_ID=$(echo "$tc"   | jq -r '.id')
      TC_NAME=$(echo "$tc" | jq -r '.function.name')
      TC_ARGS=$(echo "$tc" | jq -r '.function.arguments')
      TC_ARGS_JSON=$(echo "$TC_ARGS" | jq '.' 2>/dev/null || echo '{}')

      echo "[tool] $TC_NAME($TC_ARGS)"

      RESULT=$(_mcp_call_tool "$TC_NAME" "$TC_ARGS_JSON")
      echo "[result] ${RESULT:0:300}$([ ${#RESULT} -gt 300 ] && echo '...')"

      MESSAGES=$(echo "$MESSAGES" | jq \
        --arg id "$TC_ID" --arg content "$RESULT" \
        '. + [{"role":"tool","tool_call_id":$id,"content":$content}]')
    done <<< "$(echo "$ASST_MSG" | jq -c '.tool_calls[]?')"

    # Rate-limit guard: wait before next LLM call
    sleep "$POLL_INTERVAL"

  else
    FINAL_TEXT=$(echo "$ASST_MSG" | jq -r '.content // ""')

    # If the LLM stopped to say it's still waiting instead of polling, force it to continue
    if echo "$FINAL_TEXT" | grep -qiE 'still running|monitoring|will (wait|notify|show)|not (yet |)finish|checking back|periodic|would you like|do you want|should i (wait|continue|keep)|wait for.*to finish'; then
      MESSAGES=$(echo "$MESSAGES" | jq '. + [{"role":"user","content":"The task is still running. Do not generate text — call get_task_instance again immediately and keep polling until the task reaches a terminal state."}]')
      sleep "$POLL_INTERVAL"
      continue
    fi

    if [[ "$RAW" == true ]]; then
      echo "$RESPONSE" | jq .
    else
      echo "$FINAL_TEXT"
    fi
    exit 0
  fi
done

echo "[warn] reached max $MAX_TURNS turns without a final answer" >&2
exit 1
